content.problemStatement:
and Bengali as well.

There are NNN cats (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM rats (numbered 111 through MMM) on a line. Each cat and each rat wants to move from some point to some (possibly the same) point on this line. Naturally, the cats also want to eat the rats when they get a chance. Both the cats and the rats can only move with constant speed 111.

For each valid iii, the iii-th cat is initially sleeping at a point aia_iai‚Äč. At a time sis_isi‚Äč, this cat wakes up and starts moving to a final point bib_ibi‚Äč with constant velocity and without any detours (so it arrives at this point at the time ei=si+‚ą£ai‚ąíbi‚ą£e_i = s_i + |a_i-b_i|ei‚Äč=si‚Äč+‚ą£ai‚Äč‚ąíbi‚Äč‚ą£). After it arrives at the point bib_ibi‚Äč, it falls asleep again.

For each valid iii, the iii-th rat is initially hiding at a point cic_ici‚Äč. At a time rir_iri‚Äč, this rat stops hiding and starts moving to a final point did_idi‚Äč in the same way as the cats ‚Äē with constant velocity and without any detours, arriving at the time qi=ri+‚ą£ci‚ąídi‚ą£q_i = r_i + |c_i-d_i|qi‚Äč=ri‚Äč+‚ą£ci‚Äč‚ąídi‚Äč‚ą£ (if it does not get eaten). After it arrives at the point did_idi‚Äč, it hides again.

If a cat and a rat meet each other (they are located at the same point at the same time), the cat eats the rat, the rat disappears and cannot be eaten by any other cat. A sleeping cat cannot eat a rat and a hidden rat cannot be eaten ‚Äē formally, cat iii can eat rat jjj only if they meet at a time ttt satisfying si‚Č§t‚Č§eis_i \le t \le e_isi‚Äč‚Č§t‚Č§ei‚Äč and rj‚Č§t‚Č§qjr_j \le t \le q_jrj‚Äč‚Č§t‚Č§qj‚Äč.

Your task is to find out which rats get eaten by which cats. It is guaranteed that no two cats will meet a rat at the same time.
constraints: 1‚Č§ai,bi,si‚Č§1091 \le a_i, b_i, s_i \le 10^91‚Č§ai‚Äč,bi‚Äč,si‚Äč‚Č§109 for each valid iii
1‚Č§ci,di,ri‚Č§1091 \le c_i, d_i, r_i \le 10^91‚Č§ci‚Äč,di‚Äč,ri‚Äč‚Č§109 for each valid iii
all initial and final positions of all cats and rats are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), the iii-th of these lines contains three space-separated integers aia_iai‚Äč, bib_ibi‚Äč and sis_isi‚Äč.
MMM more lines follow. For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§M1 \le i \le M1‚Č§i‚Č§M), the iii-th of these lines contains three space-separated integers cic_ici‚Äč, did_idi‚Äč and rir_iri‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print MMM lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain a single integer ‚Äē the number of the cat that will eat the iii-th rat, or ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if no cat will eat this rat.
